title: Editas Medicine Stock Price and Financial Overview
text: Editas Medicine is a clinical stage genome editing company developing transformative genomic medicines using CRISPR/Cas12a technology. In 2025, revenue was $40.52 million, increase of 25.39% versus prior year $32.31 million, though company has never been profitable. Net losses grew from -$115.98 million in 2020 to -$237.09 million in 2024. Critical concern is cash depletion with cash and short-term investments falling from $269.91 million end of 2024 to $178.5 million mid-2025. Company reported negative free cash flow of -$50.21 million in Q2 2025 and -$47.91 million in Q1 2025, with full year 2024 FCF of -$219.11 million. At ~$50 million quarterly burn, cash runway now under one year, making company dependent on capital markets or partnerships. Lead program EDIT-401 targets LDL cholesterol reduction through LDL receptor upregulation for hyperlipidemia treatment. Preclinical data demonstrated >90% LDL-C reduction in non-human primates with IND/CTA submission planned mid-2026. Return on equity -188.58% and beta 2.42 indicate high volatility.
